name: Pitt's India Act 1784
summary: Created the 'double government' system: a Crown Board of Control exercising paramount power over political, military, and revenue affairs, alongside the Company's Court of Directors which retained commercial functions. This architecture governed India until 1858.
key_provisions: Board of Control — six members including two Cabinet ministers
Board has authority over all civil, military, and revenue dispatches
Secret Committee of three Directors for urgent dispatches
Governor-General appointed by Crown on Directors' nomination
Prohibition on offensive wars without Board approval
Corrupt acts of servants triable in English courts
demise: Superseded by the Government of India Act 1858.
